codeReview.summary: Fail. Commit `1b431eb` landed the mocked hosted-provider create/resume and auth/state/capability slice, and the transcript/handoff show the intended targeted tests and workspace-wide validation were run. However, the current PTY-capability reporting does not match the feature contract: supported providers only report PTY support when the session was created with `interactive_pty = true`, so capability preservation is tied to a prior request flag instead of the backend's actual support.

issues.description: `supports_pty()` returns `SPEC.supports_pty && self.state.interactive_pty`, which means E2B, Daytona, Blaxel, and Cloudflare sessions created without requesting interactive PTY incorrectly report that PTY is unsupported. Upstream hosted-provider sessions expose PTY support based on backend capability (for example E2B/Daytona/Cloudflare return `true` regardless of the initial interactive flag), so this violates the feature's expected behavior that supported providers preserve PTY capability flags while unsupported providers fail clearly.

The capability regression test only asserts `supports_pty()` for an E2B session created with `interactive_pty: true` and for a Vercel session that is always unsupported. It never checks a PTY-capable provider created with default/non-interactive options, so the false-negative capability report above slips through.

summary: Reviewed the mission feature record, AGENTS/services/architecture guidance, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `1b431eb`, then inspected the current hosted-provider sandbox implementation and tests. The feature landed in `1b431eb`, but the current PTY-capability contract is wrong for supported providers because `supports_pty()` is gated by the session's `interactive_pty` state instead of backend support, so I marked the review as fail with one blocking issue and one related test-gap note.
